verdict_reasoning: Fibronectin (FN1) is a large glycoprotein secreted into plasma (soluble dimeric form) and deposited into the extracellular matrix as multimeric fibrils. It lacks a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or outer-leaflet lipidation. The NCBI summary notes both a plasma form and a cell-surface/ECM form, but ECM deposition is matrix-anchored covalent/non-covalent deposition — not stable attachment to a TM partner on the plasma membrane outer leaflet in the required sense. Pre-no checklist: (1) no cell-surface-directed antibody/ADC/CAR-T program against the FN1 protein body; (2) not a sheddase TM precursor — no TM domain in any isoform; (3) ECM fibrils are matrix-deposited, not wash-resistant TM-partner-tethered surface display; (4) no documented PM minority pool distinct from ECM deposition; (5) literature evidence (PMIDs 36812915, 39805388, 40734271) confirms secreted/ECM biology, no surface-biotinylation or flow-cytometry evidence of outer-leaflet display. All five contextual buckets lack gene-specific outer-leaflet evidence.
